pr.title: Fix CVE-2026-44289: Pin protobufjs to 7.5.6
pr.body:
## Security Fix: CVE-2026-44289

This PR addresses **CVE-2026-44289** in the `protobufjs` package.

### Details
- **Package:** `protobufjs`
- **Vulnerable version:** `7.5.5`
- **Fixed versions:** `7.5.6`, `8.0.2`
- **Resolved version:** `7.5.6` (the minimum patched 7.x release)

### Dependency type
`protobufjs` is a **transitive dependency** pulled in by `@opentelemetry/otlp-transformer` (which requires `protobufjs: ^7.3.0`). It is not listed directly in `frontend/package.json`.

### Changes
- `frontend/package-lock.json`: pinned `protobufjs` from `7.5.5` → `7.5.6` (matches the patched version exactly).

We initially bumped to `7.6.0` (the latest 7.x), but per reviewer feedback we pinned back to `7.5.6` — `7.6.0` was only two days old and did not pass the supply-chain maturity guardrail. `7.5.6` is the exact upstream patch for the CVE, so it is sufficient to resolve the advisory without taking on a brand-new minor release.

No `package.json` changes were necessary — the pin is purely at the lockfile level, keeping the dependency set minimal.

### Verification
```
$ grep -A2 '"node_modules/protobufjs"' frontend/package-lock.json | head -3
"node_modules/protobufjs": {
  "version": "7.5.6",
  "resolved": "https://registry.npmjs.org/protobufjs/-/protobufjs-7.5.6.tgz",
```

---
_This PR was created by an AI agent (OpenHands) on behalf of the maintainers as part of automated CVE remediation._

---

To run this PR locally, use the following command:

GUI with Docker:
```
docker run -it --rm   -p 3000:3000   -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock   --add-host host.docker.internal:host-gateway   --name openhands-app-00cef69   docker.openhands.dev/openhands/openhands:00cef69
```
pr.author: aivong-openhands
